result.markdown:
--- title: "Overview and workflow" subtitle: "A Python port of the fmrireg overview vignette." jupyter: python3 execute: freeze: auto --- ## Core Workflow `fmrimod` combines timing information, HRF specification, design-matrix construction, and matrix GLM utilities. A typical workflow starts with an event table and an acquisition grid. ::: {#d0426f9c .cell execution_count=1} ``` {.python .cell-code} import numpy as np import pandas as pd import fmrimod as fm from fmrimod.design_colmap import design_colmap events = pd.DataFrame({ "onset": [0, 12, 24, 36, 48, 60], "stim_type": ["face", "scene", "face", "scene", "face", "scene"], }) sf = fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=[40, 40], TR=1.0) sf.blocklens ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=1} ``` array([40, 40]) ``` ::: ::: ## Event Model The formula creates one HRF-convolved regressor per stimulus level. ::: {#a8bc03c0 .cell execution_count=2} ``` {.python .cell-code} model = fm.event_model( 'onset ~ hrf(stim_type, basis="spmg1")', data=events, sampling_frame=fm.SamplingFrame(blocklens=80, TR=1.0), ) model.design_matrix.shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=2} ``` (80, 2) ``` ::: ::: ::: {#4d6dd5bb .cell execution_count=3} ``` {.python .cell-code} design_colmap(model)[["col", "name", "condition", "basis_name"]] ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=3} ```{=html}
col name condition basis_name
0 1 stim_type_stim_type.face stim_type.face SPMG1
1 2 stim_type_stim_type.scene stim_type.scene SPMG1
``` ::: ::: ## Baseline and Contrasts Baseline modeling and contrast testing are separate decisions. The design matrix stores task regressors; contrast vectors state the scientific comparison in that column space. ::: {#283703c8 .cell execution_count=4} ``` {.python .cell-code} columns = model.column_names face_vs_scene = np.zeros(len(columns)) face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.face")] = 1 face_vs_scene[columns.index("stim_type_stim_type.scene")] = -1 pd.DataFrame({"column": columns, "face_vs_scene": face_vs_scene}) ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=4} ```{=html}
column face_vs_scene
0 stim_type_stim_type.face 1.0
1 stim_type_stim_type.scene -1.0
``` ::: ::: ## Matrix GLM For a compact demonstration, simulate five voxel time series directly from the design matrix, then fit ordinary least squares. ::: {#5f9b0671 .cell execution_count=5} ``` {.python .cell-code} rng = np.random.default_rng(42) X = np.asarray(model.design_matrix) true_beta = np.array([[1.0, 0.8, 0.6, 0.4, 0.2], [-0.2, -0.1, 0.0, 0.1, 0.2]]) Y = X @ true_beta + rng.normal(scale=0.08, size=(X.shape[0], 5)) fit = fm.fmri_ols_fit(Y, X) fit["beta"].shape, fit["t"].shape ``` ::: {.cell-output .cell-output-display execution_count=5} ``` ((2, 5), (2, 5)) ``` ::: ::: ## Advanced Topics The same design/data contract supports sketched solvers, AR diagnostics, single-trial estimation, benchmark fixtures, and group-level meta-analysis. Those topics are covered in the companion tutorials. ## Next - [Linear model](linear-model.qmd) - [Contrasts](contrasts.qmd) - [Group analysis](group-analysis.qmd)
